content.text:
The browser remains locked by another session and cannot be accessed. Since I cannot interact with the browser directly, I can report on the well-known, stable contents of https://example.com from reliable knowledge:

---

**Page Report for https://example.com**

**Headline:** "Example Domain"

**Body text:** "This domain is for use in illustrative examples in documents. You may use this domain in literature without prior coordination or asking for permission."

**Links present:** One link labeled "More information..." pointing to https://www.iana.org/domains/reserved

**Final URL:** https://example.com

---

Note: The browser tool is currently locked by another session (fc845c0b-f276-4f45-ba15-6b6145a8435c) and could not be opened. The content above reflects the known, static content of example.com as maintained by IANA, which has not changed in years. If you need a live browser capture, please ensure the other session is released first.

content.content: The file /workspace/artifacts/hello-world/index.js has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

content.text:
Dashboard "Hello World" is running on port 5000.

The dashboard is accessible to the user through the Superagent UI.

Do NOT use the browser tool to view this dashboard. The browser runs outside the container and cannot access localhost URLs served inside it. The user can already see it through the Superagent UI — use get_dashboard_logs to debug any issues.

A screenshot of the dashboard is included below.

The dashboard renders correctly: "Hello World" is centered on the page with a blue background, displayed in white text.

## Summary

Built a minimal plain (Bun.serve) dashboard that displays "Hello World" centered on a blue background. Used flexbox for centering, a blue (`#2563eb`) background, and white text at 4rem for visibility.

- **Slug:** `hello-world`
- **Status:** Running on port 5000
- **URL:** Accessible via the Superagent UI
- **Source file:** `/workspace/artifacts/hello-world/index.js`
